explanations.1: This special comment tells Docker to use a specific syntax version for parsing the Dockerfile, which enables newer Docker features.
explanations.30: This RUN command uses a cache mount, which is a Docker feature that preserves downloaded packages between builds so you don't have to re-download them every time.
explanations.45: This RUN command uses both a cache mount for pip packages and a bind mount to read requirements.txt without copying it into the image layer.
explanations.76: The addgroup command is being run inside the container; it creates a user group with a specific ID so permissions work correctly between your host machine and container.
explanations.78: The adduser command creates a new user account inside the container with special flags: --system means it's a system user, --disabled-password means it has no password, and --gecos "" skips the usual user info prompt.
explanations.92: This long bash command customizes the shell prompt (the text that appears when you open a terminal) to show an emoji, container name, and who you are logged in as.
explanations.98: This SNIPPET variable stores shell commands that get added to .bashrc to make bash history persist to a file, so your command history survives when the container restarts.
